name: flow/flow-node
description: An HTML node box positioned in the viewport pane — draggable, selectable, with arbitrary children.

meta.name: FlowNode
meta.description: An HTML node box positioned in the viewport pane — draggable, selectable, with arbitrary children.

props.name: zoom
props.description: Current zoom (drag deltas are divided by it).

props.name: selected
props.description: Whether the node is rendered as selected.

props.name: draggable
props.description: Whether the node can be dragged.

props.name: interactive
props.description: When false, the node is view-only: not draggable, selectable, or focusable.

props.name: className
props.description: Additional CSS class names merged onto the root element.

examples.title: A draggable node
examples.code:
() => (
Service A
)

whenToUse: Representing a unit in a graph that holds arbitrary themed content
When you hand-compose a flow and need draggable, selectable boxes
whenNotToUse: For the common case — the declarative <Flow> renders nodes for you

antiPatterns.bad: Rendering node content into an SVG
antiPatterns.why: HTML nodes inherit the data-theme scope and can contain any cascivo component.

related.name: FlowHandle
related.reason: Connection ports live inside a node.

intent.a11yRationale: Focusable group; Enter/Space select. Visual states (hover/focus/selected) are CSS.
